title: Egoist Tutorial Nr.1 Overview
transcript: [Music] hey this is Luke from bg.com and I'm really excited today to be able to demonstrate to you a brand new product from sugar bites and the name of this new device is called egoist so let's take a look at it so you may be wondering exactly what is egoist and what can it do well egoist combines a slicer with a bead and a base section and each of these three engines can be routed through an effect sequencer so you can load up a example you can slice it up and arrange it pick a beat and add some base and then use up to seven different effects to finish it off the Simplicity and the limitations of egoist are really going to channel your creativity and anything you do within egoist is absolutely immediate so you can change things on the go while the loop is running and there's plenty of intelligent random buttons and your ear will know when it sounds good if you're not happy with it then you can just keep playing and adjusting parameters until you find something that sounds really good so the play button on the the top left corner is going to start egoist if I click here there's a drop down menu and let's go into Rico and we're going to start off by selecting a random preset so let's choose petent and we can cycle between preloaded loops you can hear that the drums sound completely different now it's using different samples so let's keep it un peted for now so we have this Tempo control here and by default it's set to 116th with this particular Loop and change it to 1/8 in/ thir or back to 11/16 I can also control the swing amount as a percentage here so at 28% swing you can really hear that that's got some Groove in it now and then moving to the right from there we have the slicer basic controls so we can deactivate the slicer so all you can hear is the beat currently if I reactivate the slicer we can control the level of it here and I should also add that I'm using the as a standalone device but if you loaded this up inside your digital audio workstation as a VST plugin then you could draw automations within your door to control parameters any parameter such as the level fading up fading out we also have the bass and then the beat you can just turn the beat off or control the level of it and then you can control the overall volume here to the left we can see that we have these different tabs so let's walk you through those before we look at them in depth in further videos first we have the slicer window and the slicer window controls the sample you can choose samples from the menu that I showed you here or we can actually load up our own samples and slice them up which I'll demonstrate later next we have the b/ beat window so you can program a baseline you can program beats here you can really dial in the sound of your bass tones and also your individual drums get a lot of control over them here and then we have the effects tab where you have seven different effects and you can see that as we move these different parameters visually we see things moving so this is really really easy and intuitive and these effects sound absolutely great they're very very high-grade very professional sounding finally we have the egoist window where you get a lot more controls and then down at the bottom here you can see that we have these different 16 different buttons here that control different patterns or different parts or the whole song so you could see on the right we have a green but that says pattern and this is where we would program our patterns whether it be effects patterns base and beat patterns or slicer patterns you can then create Parts which are made up of different patterns so one part May consist of four patterns or six patterns or however many you choose and then we can go into this song Tab and we can program an entire song made up from parts so you could actually write an entire track using just egoist and you can really get a lot of control over it if you want to to make it sound exactly how you want or you could just play with it randomly and in a lot more of a an open and experimental way so this has been an overview of the interface and in the next video we're going to get more in depth and start looking at the slicer window so I'll see you in the next video
